releases.title: v3.4.3
releases.summary: This patch cuts the Art library read path over to native Art storage while keeping bridge-compatible response ids during the transition. Events now read tracked Art purchases and tracked Collectibles purchases through the shared purchased-item relationship, and the Art drawer treats vendor and booth as event purchase context instead of always-on Art identity fields.

releases.title: v3.4.2
releases.summary: This patch migrates existing bridge-era Art data into the native Art storage introduced in `3.4.1`. Existing `collectibles.subtype = art` rows now receive native `art_items` mappings, and event-linked Art purchases now receive shared `event_purchased_items` links so Events can carry Art purchase history through the same canonical relationship used for tracked purchased objects.

releases.title: v3.4.1
releases.summary: This patch builds the foundation for separating Art from Collectibles without forcing an immediate read cutover. Art still works through the bridge-era `/api/art` surface, but new and edited Art records now dual-write into dedicated native Art storage. Events also gain a shared purchased-item relationship so tracked purchases can link to either native Art rows or non-Art Collectibles through one event-facing contract.

releases.title: v3.4.0
releases.summary: This minor release promotes Art into its own first-class library surface while preserving event-linked purchase history and cleaning up the remaining Collectibles entry friction that no longer made sense once Art stopped living only as a subtype. The release keeps the underlying bridge model intentionally pragmatic: Art now has dedicated routes and navigation, shared collectible-style purchase linkage still works, Art metadata grows into a more natural shape with `series`, `vendor`, and `booth`, and the Collectibles drawer collapses its duplicate taxonomy controls into one clearer selector.

releases.title: v3.3.5
releases.summary: This patch release rounds out the current library-loans reminder line by adding an operator-facing reminder-operations surface. The release keeps the scope intentionally narrow: instead of creating a separate monitoring dashboard, it adds a dedicated admin read contract and a compact panel inside the existing platform activity view so operators can see reminder runtime state, latest automatic run results, and recent failures in one place.

releases.title: v3.3.4
releases.summary: This patch release broadens the shipped library-loans workflow by bringing title-level loan history into the dedicated `Loans` workspace. The release keeps the implementation intentionally narrow: the main loans list stays lean, while each row can expand into per-title current and returned history with attached reminder-event context. The result is a clearer management view for past loans without turning the workspace into a full operator audit console.

releases.title: v3.3.3
releases.summary: This patch release deepens the shipped library-loans reminder workflow by adding event-level reminder history, exposing that history through the loan-detail API, and surfacing compact reminder audit context in the loan-first media drawer. The release keeps the design intentionally narrow: reminder history is now explainable and reviewable without widening the main Loans list into a heavier operator console.

releases.title: v3.3.2
releases.summary: This patch release builds on `3.3.1` loan reminders by adding automatic reminder scheduling for due-soon and overdue loans. The shipped scope keeps the implementation deliberately tight: a shared backend reminder service, a lightweight in-process scheduler, phase-aware duplicate prevention, and Docker-backed proof that automatic sweeps send the right reminders without repeating them immediately.

releases.title: v3.3.1
releases.summary: This patch release builds on `3.3.0` library loans tracking by adding manual loan reminders, clearer reminder status in the drawer and Loans view, a more accurate due-soon count, and focused browser coverage for the loan-first drawer flow. The result is a tighter follow-up workflow for items that are due soon or already overdue without widening the release into automatic scheduling.

releases.title: v3.3.0
releases.summary: This minor release adds a new library loans workflow so borrowed items can be tracked from one management surface instead of disappearing into title-level notes. The release introduces scoped loan records, item-attached loan actions, a dedicated `Loans` view, and stronger current-loan visibility for active, overdue, and returned items across both platform and homelab stacks.
